summary: End-to-end IAM DB-Token Spark JDBC read on a fresh provisioned ALH/ATP instance. Token issued locally via OCI identity_data_plane.generate_scoped_access_token (scope = compartment AIDP_TPC-DS_Ahmed), staged on driver + 8 cluster executors, JDBC driver presented token, ALH validated against the IAM mapping and returned the row.
what_validated: OCI_IAM external auth feature enabled on ALH (DBMS_CLOUD_ADMIN.ENABLE_EXTERNAL_AUTHENTICATION type=>'OCI_IAM')
DB user mapped to IDCS principal: CREATE USER iam_test_user IDENTIFIED GLOBALLY AS 'IAM_PRINCIPAL_NAME=oracleidentitycloudservice/ahmed.shahzad.awan@oracle.com'
Spark DataFrame read with executor distribution (broadcast token+key+wallet to 64 partitions on 8 executors)
key_lessons: ADB ships with identity_provider_type=NONE by default. ENABLE_EXTERNAL_AUTHENTICATION type=>'OCI_IAM' (NOT ENABLE_FEATURE) flips it to OCI_IAM.
ADB ADMIN cannot ALTER SYSTEM SET identity_provider_type — must use the DBMS_CLOUD_ADMIN procedure.

For IDCS-federated users (JWT field domain_name=oracleidentitycloudservice), the IAM_PRINCIPAL_NAME mapping must include the domain prefix: 'oracleidentitycloudservice/<email>'. Plain '<email>' alone returns ORA-01017.
Oracle JDBC OCI_TOKEN auth requires BOTH /tmp/dbcred_alh/token AND /tmp/dbcred_alh/oci_db_key.pem (the private key matching the JWT's public_key claim) — already encoded in the dbtoken.py helper.
skill_correctness: scripts/oracle_ai_data_platform_connectors/auth/dbtoken.py generate_db_token() validated end-to-end. spark_jdbc_options_dbtoken() builder produces the right JDBC properties.
test_database_disposition: ALHTEST instance created in compartment AIDP_TPC-DS_Ahmed. Keep until row 3 also passes, then delete to avoid ongoing cost.
